description: Senior-engineering audit follow-ups: remove leaky abstractions, kill dead code paths, dedupe UI, and tighten types across the pipeline and SPA.

userStories.title: Replace catalog/allRules Proxy exports with explicit getters
userStories.description: As a pipeline maintainer, I want catalog access to be explicit and safe so new sync callers can't silently trigger the 'aggregator not warmed' trap and so iteration doesn't pay per-call Proxy bind overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/catalog.ts no longer exports `tagCatalog` as a Proxy; replace with `getTagCatalog(): readonly TagDef[]` (or equivalent named export) backed by the aggregator's cached snapshot
pipeline/rules/index.ts no longer exports `allRules` as a Proxy; replace with `getAllRules(): readonly Rule[]`
All call sites in pipeline/index.ts, pipeline/graph.ts, pipeline/coverage.ts, and pipeline/scripts/* updated to use the new functions
RULE_VERSION export from pipeline/catalog.ts is preserved
`npm run test:pipeline` passes
`npm run build:cards -- --set tdm` writes a valid artifact (smoke check that no caller broke)
Typecheck passes (`npm --prefix app run build` for app TS; pipeline TS is exercised by vitest)
userStories.notes: Replaced Proxy-based `tagCatalog` and `allRules` exports with explicit `getTagCatalog()` and `getAllRules()` getters in pipeline/catalog.ts and pipeline/rules/index.ts. Both delegate to the aggregator's cached sync snapshot. Updated call sites in pipeline/index.ts, pipeline/e2e.test.ts, and pipeline/catalog.test.ts. RULE_VERSION export preserved. pipeline/graph.ts (takes catalog as a parameter), pipeline/coverage.ts (no references), and pipeline/scripts/* (already used aggregator directly) needed no changes. Verified: npm run test:pipeline — 1315/1315 pass; npm run build:cards -- --set tdm — emits valid artifact with 4275 edges; npm --prefix app run build — typecheck + vite build succeed.

userStories.title: Pre-compute tribe-type map in buildEdges instead of per-pair tag scan
userStories.description: As a pipeline maintainer, I want the tribe-edge gate in graph.ts to use an O(1) lookup so the (source × target) join doesn't re-scan source.tags for every cares_tribe.* target.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/graph.ts builds `Map<oracleId, Set<creatureType>>` once before the pairing loop, derived from each card's `effect.create_creature_token` metadata.creatureTypes
The `consumerId.startsWith('condition.cares_tribe.')` branch reads from that pre-computed map instead of calling `cardsByOracleId.get(source)?.tags.find(...)` per iteration
Standard build edge count is unchanged (compare before/after `npm run build:cards -- --standard` edge counts in stdout)
Pipeline tests pass (`npm run test:pipeline`)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pre-computed `tribesByOracleId: Map<oracleId, Set<creatureType>>` inside the existing per-card tag-indexing loop in pipeline/graph.ts (no extra pass over cards). Aggregates `metadata.creatureTypes` from every `effect.create_creature_token` tag on each card (defensive union — current rule only emits one such tag per card, but a Set tolerates duplicates without changing edge count). Tribe-edge gate now does `tribesByOracleId.get(source)?.has(tribe)` instead of `cardsByOracleId.get(source)?.tags.find(...)`. Dropped the no-longer-used `cardsByOracleId` Map. Extracted the `'condition.cares_tribe.'` literal to a `TRIBE_PREFIX` const. Verified: npm run test:pipeline — 1315/1315 pass; npm run build:cards -- --standard — 748281 edges (identical to baseline); npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed.

userStories.title: Simplify stripReminderText to a single-pass replace
userStories.description: As a pipeline maintainer, I want normalizeOracleText's helpers to be straightforward so future edits don't drift between the two regex passes.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/normalize.ts:stripReminderText runs the `\([^)]*\)` strip exactly once, then branches on the result
Existing normalize tests pass without modification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Refactored stripReminderText in pipeline/normalize.ts to strip `\([^)]*\)` exactly once, store the result in `stripped`, then branch on the whitespace-collapsed/trimmed length. Both branches (empty-check and final-return) now read from the single `stripped` value instead of re-running the paren regex. Behavior is identical: empty-check still uses `\s{2,}` collapse + trim; final return still uses `[ \t]+\.` collapse + trim. Verified: existing pipeline/normalize.test.ts (8 tests) passes unchanged; npm run test:pipeline — 1315/1315 pass; npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed.

userStories.title: Validate expandChildren child references against the catalog
userStories.description: As a pipeline maintainer, I want expandChildren to fail loudly when a child id is missing from the catalog instead of silently emitting a ghost tag with the parent's axis.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/tag-expansion.ts throws (or logs and skips) when `catalog[childId]` is missing, instead of pushing a CardTag with the parent's axis
Add a unit test covering the missing-child case in pipeline/tag-expansion.test.ts
Pipeline tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: expandChildren in pipeline/tag-expansion.ts now throws `Error: expandChildren: parent <parentId> declares child <childId> which is not in the catalog` when catalog[childId] is missing, instead of pushing a ghost CardTag with the parent's axis. childDef.axis is now read directly (no `?? tag.axis` fallback) so the child's axis is always its own axis from the catalog. The catalog consistency test (pipeline/catalog.test.ts: 'every child id referenced in a parent.children list exists in the catalog') already prevents this at build time, so the throw is a defensive runtime check that surfaces drift loudly. Updated pipeline/tag-expansion.test.ts: replaced the legacy 'ignores ... gracefully' case with 'throws when a parent declares a child id missing from the catalog' (still 5 tests total in the file). Verified: npm run test:pipeline — 1315/1315 pass; npm run build:cards -- --set tdm — 277 cards / 271 tagged / 4275 edges (matches baseline); npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed.

userStories.title: Move hasCachedSet declaration below the import block in pipeline/index.ts
userStories.description: As a pipeline maintainer, I want imports grouped together at the top of the file so it reads consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/index.ts: all `import` statements appear before any function declarations
`hasCachedSet` is defined once, in a single contiguous declaration block after imports
Pipeline tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Moved `hasCachedSet` function declaration in pipeline/index.ts from its previous position (sandwiched between two import groups at lines 10-12) to a single contiguous declaration block immediately after all imports. All 12 import statements now appear contiguously at the top of the file (lines 2-16); `hasCachedSet` follows at lines 18-20, then the `Args` type, then `parseArgs`/`tagCards`/`main`. Behavior is unchanged — the function body and its single caller in `main()` are untouched. Verified: npm run test:pipeline — 134 files, 1315/1315 tests pass; npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed.

userStories.title: Type tag metadata via a per-tag TagMetadataMap
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CardTag.metadata to be typed by tagId so consumers like graph.ts don't need bare `as { creatureTypes?: string[] }` casts.
acceptanceCriteria: shared/types.ts introduces a `TagMetadataMap` interface keyed by tagId (initially with at least `'effect.create_creature_token': { creatureTypes: string[] }`)

`CardTag` is updated so `metadata` is typed against `TagMetadataMap[TagId] | undefined` (use a generic or discriminated union — pick the approach that compiles cleanly with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`)
pipeline/graph.ts reads `tokenTag.metadata.creatureTypes` without an `as` cast
Existing runner code that emits `metadata` (e.g. pipeline/rules/effect.create_creature_token.ts) still compiles and tests pass
Pipeline tests pass
Typecheck passes for both pipeline and app
userStories.notes: Added `TagMetadataMap` interface to shared/types.ts with the initial `'effect.create_creature_token': { creatureTypes: string[] }` entry. Made `CardTag` generic over `TagId extends string = string`; the `metadata` field is typed conditionally as `TagMetadataMap[TagId]` when the id is in the map, else `Record<string, unknown>`. Default parameter preserves backward compat — every existing `CardTag` (no generic argument) call site keeps the same shape it had before. Added a `hasTagId<T extends keyof TagMetadataMap>(tag, tagId): tag is CardTag<T>` predicate so consumers can narrow without an `as` cast. pipeline/graph.ts now uses `if (hasTagId(t, 'effect.create_creature_token'))` and reads `t.metadata?.creatureTypes` (typed `string[] | undefined`) directly — the `as { creatureTypes?: string[] }` cast is gone. pipeline/rules/effect.create_creature_token.ts and pipeline/rules/runner.ts continue to compile unchanged because they construct CardTag<string> (default) where metadata is the open `Record<string, unknown>`. Verified: npm run test:pipeline — 134 files / 1315 tests pass (includes shared/types.test.ts: `expectTypeOf(c.tags).toEqualTypeOf<CardTag[]>()` still matches since the generic defaults to string); npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed; npm run build:cards -- --set tdm — 277 cards / 271 tagged / 4275 edges (byte-identical baseline).

userStories.title: Wire artifactCache reads into graphStore.hydrate with ruleVersion check
userStories.description: As a user revisiting the app, I want the previously-fetched artifact to load from IndexedDB instead of re-downloading multi-MB JSON on every page load, but only when ruleVersion still matches.
acceptanceCriteria: app/src/stores/graphStore.ts:hydrate first attempts `db.artifactCache.toArray()` (or a keyed lookup) and uses the cached artifact if its `ruleVersion` matches the current `RULE_VERSION` AND its `sourceSet` matches the requested set
On cache miss / ruleVersion mismatch / sourceSet mismatch, fall back to the existing `fetch(url)` path and write the result to cache
Stale cache rows (different ruleVersion) are deleted on hydration to avoid unbounded growth
Add a unit test in app/src/stores/graphStore.test.ts covering both cache-hit and cache-miss paths (use fake-indexeddb, which is already loaded in app/tests/setup.ts)
App tests pass (`npm --prefix app test`)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: load the app, confirm it renders; reload, confirm the network panel shows the JSON fetch is skipped on the second load
userStories.notes: Hoisted RULE_VERSION from pipeline/catalog.ts to a new shared/version.ts so the app can import it without depending on pipeline internals; pipeline/catalog.ts now re-exports RULE_VERSION from shared/version, so every existing pipeline import path keeps working unchanged. app/src/stores/graphStore.ts:hydrate now reads db.artifactCache.toArray() first; if a row exists with ruleVersion === RULE_VERSION AND sourceSet === <set parsed from the URL via /cards-([^/]+)\.json(?:\?|$)/>, the artifact is applied from cache and fetch is skipped entirely. Stale rows (any ruleVersion !== RULE_VERSION) are deleted via bulkDelete before the hit lookup so the IndexedDB store stays bounded across rule-version bumps. Cache-miss path falls through to the existing fetch + .put flow (the .put is now awaited but still .catch(() => undefined) so a DB-write failure can't break hydration — the await makes the write-back test deterministic). Extracted cards/edges/edgesInbound/tagCatalog Map construction into a local applyArtifact(artifact, set) helper shared by both paths. Added 4 unit tests in graphStore.test.ts: cache-hit (fetch never called), cache-miss write-back (row written with matching ruleVersion+sourceSet), stale-row eviction (v0.0.0-old row removed, only RULE_VERSION row remains), and sourceSet mismatch (refetches because cache is per-set). beforeEach is now async and clears db.artifactCache between tests so fake-indexeddb doesn't leak rows across cases. Verified: npm --prefix app test — 22 files / 133 tests pass (was 129; +4 new cache tests); npm test — pipeline 134 files / 1315 tests + app 22 files / 133 tests + vite build all pass; npm run build:cards -- --set tdm — 277 cards / 271 tagged / 4275 edges, artifact ruleVersion: v0.9.7 / sourceSet: tdm. Browser-side reload smoke test (dev-browser skill) was NOT performed — that skill isn't loaded in this session; unit tests cover both paths under fake-indexeddb but a manual reload in Chrome is worth doing before tagging a release.

userStories.title: Log hydration errors in graphStore instead of swallowing them
userStories.description: As a developer debugging a failed app load, I want the actual error in the console so I'm not guessing whether it was network, parse, or schema.
acceptanceCriteria: graphStore.hydrate's `catch` block calls `console.error('[graphStore] hydrate failed:', err)` before setting status to 'error'
The non-OK response branch also logs the status/statusText
Existing graphStore tests still pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added two console.error calls in app/src/stores/graphStore.ts:hydrate. The non-OK response branch now logs `[graphStore] hydrate failed: response not ok` with status + statusText before flipping status to 'error'. The catch block now binds the error (was bare `catch {}`) and logs `[graphStore] hydrate failed:` with the err object before flipping status to 'error'. Updated the existing 'sets status to error on fetch failure' test in graphStore.test.ts to (a) include a `statusText` on the mocked 500 response, (b) spy on console.error with mockImplementation to suppress noise in test output, and (c) assert the spy was called with the expected args — so the log is now covered by the regression test, not just incidental. No new test file needed; the existing fetch-failure case naturally exercises the non-OK branch and the catch branch isn't trivially testable without breaking JSON parsing (deferred — the assertion on the non-OK branch is enough to lock in the behavior). Verified: npm --prefix app test — 22 files / 129 tests pass; npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed.

userStories.title: Move data hydration from main.tsx into a React effect
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want graph + deck loading to live inside the React tree so we can later add retry, Suspense, or an error boundary without rewriting bootstrap.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the top-level `useGraphStore.getState().hydrate(...)` and `useDeckStore.getState().load()` calls from app/src/main.tsx
Add a `useEffect` in app/src/App.tsx (or a small `<DataLoader>` wrapper inside it) that calls both on mount
ARTIFACT_URL resolution (VITE_SET_CODE handling) moves with the hydrate call
App tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: app loads card data and renders the browser grid as before
userStories.notes: Removed top-level `useGraphStore.getState().hydrate(ARTIFACT_URL)` and `useDeckStore.getState().load()` calls from app/src/main.tsx, along with the `ARTIFACT_URL` IIFE and the two store imports. main.tsx is now a pure ReactDOM mount + global CSS imports. Moved ARTIFACT_URL resolution and both store imports into app/src/App.tsx; added a single `useEffect(() => { useGraphStore.getState().hydrate(ARTIFACT_URL); useDeckStore.getState().load(); }, [])` at the top of the App component. Kept ARTIFACT_URL as a module-level const (evaluated once at module load) rather than recomputing per render — VITE_SET_CODE is fixed at build time, so there's no reactive dependency to track. Used getState() inside the effect (not the hook) so the effect deps array stays empty and the effect doesn't re-fire when store state changes. StrictMode double-invokes effects in dev, but both calls are idempotent: hydrate's first action sets status to 'loading' and the cache lookup (US-007) makes the second call cheap; load() just reads decks from Dexie. Verified: npm --prefix app test — 22 files / 133 tests pass; npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed; npm run test:pipeline — 134 files / 1315 tests pass. Browser smoke test (dev-browser skill) NOT performed — same caveat as US-007.

userStories.title: Extract useActiveDeck hook and replace duplicated selectors
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want one canonical way to get the active deck so the same `s.activeDeckId ? s.decks.find(...) ?? null : null` selector isn't pasted across 5+ components.
acceptanceCriteria: New `useActiveDeck()` hook in app/src/stores/deckStore.ts (or app/src/lib/useActiveDeck.ts) returning the active `Deck | null`
DeckPanel.tsx, DeckPanelCollapsed.tsx, InteractionsPanel.tsx, FilterPanel.tsx, and AddToDeckButton.tsx all use the new hook instead of inline selectors
Hook is memoized so subscribers don't re-render when an unrelated deck mutates (verify by inspecting the selector or adding a small test)
App tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: deck panel renders, AddToDeckButton reflects current count, filters still show pinned themes
userStories.notes: Added `useActiveDeck(): Deck | null` hook to app/src/stores/deckStore.ts (after the useDeckStore create call). The hook wraps the same `s.activeDeckId ? s.decks.find((d) => d.id === s.activeDeckId) ?? null : null` selector that was duplicated across 5 components. Reference stability is inherited from the underlying store's mutation pattern: every mutation that touches a deck (renameDeck, addCard, removeCard) uses `decks.map((d) => d.id === id ? { ...d } : d)`, so unrelated decks keep their object identity, `find` returns the same Deck object, and Zustand's default Object.is comparison short-circuits the re-render. Updated 5 call sites: DeckPanel.tsx, DeckPanelCollapsed.tsx, InteractionsPanel.tsx, FilterPanel.tsx, AddToDeckButton.tsx. AddToDeckButton previously had a derived inline selector (`useDeckStore((s) => { if (!s.activeDeckId) return 0; const d = s.decks.find(...); return d?.cards.find(...)?.count ?? 0; })`); now it reads `activeDeck = useActiveDeck()` once and derives `activeDeckId` + `count` synchronously from that. Added 4 new tests in deckStore.test.ts (new `describe('useActiveDeck')` block using `renderHook` from @testing-library/react): null when no active deck, null when activeDeckId doesn't resolve, returns the matched deck, AND a memoization test that mutates an unrelated deck and asserts both same Deck reference AND no extra component re-render. Verified: npm --prefix app test — 22 files / 137 tests pass (was 133; +4 new); npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed; npm run test:pipeline — 134 files / 1315 tests pass. Browser smoke test (dev-browser skill) NOT performed — same caveat as US-007/US-009; the unit tests cover the underlying selector behavior and no JSX changed in any of the 5 consumers.

userStories.title: Clean up InteractionsPanel inner closures and remove eslint-disable
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the InteractionsPanel useMemo deps to be honest so a future change can't introduce a stale-closure bug.
acceptanceCriteria: Lift `reasonCategory`, `tagCounts`, and `applyColorCmc` out of the component (as top-level pure helpers taking `tagCatalog` / `cards` / `filter` as args) OR wrap them in `useCallback` with explicit deps
Remove the `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` comment in InteractionsPanel.tsx
`npm --prefix app run lint` (or the project's lint script) passes with no exhaustive-deps warnings on this file
InteractionsPanel tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open a card with both interaction and theme edges, both tabs show the correct neighbor lists and the type-chips above filter correctly
userStories.notes: Lifted three closures out of InteractionsPanel as top-level pure helpers in app/src/components/InteractionsPanel.tsx: `reasonCategory(tagCatalog, sourceTagId, targetTagId)`, `tagCounts(neighbors)`, and `applyColorCmc(neighbors, cards, filter)`. Also extracted a fourth helper `splitByCategory(neighbors, tagCatalog)` that wraps the per-neighbor reason-filter loop (previously inlined in the useMemo body that the eslint-disable was annotating); pulling it out removed the implicit `reasonCategory` closure dependency that made the deps array dishonest. The three useMemo deps arrays are now byte-honest — every value the body reads is listed. Removed the `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` comment. Imports updated: added `type Neighbor` from ../lib/traversal (so helpers can type their inputs without `typeof neighbors`), added `Card` and `TagDef` from @shared/types. Component body is now shorter and the closures are testable in isolation if a future story needs that. Verified: npm --prefix app test -- InteractionsPanel — 5/5 pass; npm --prefix app test — 22 files / 137 tests pass; npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed; npm run test:pipeline — 134 files / 1315 tests pass; npm run lint output for InteractionsPanel.tsx — no exhaustive-deps warnings (the react-hooks/exhaustive-deps rule isn't actually wired into .eslintrc.cjs since eslint-plugin-react-hooks isn't installed, so the original comment was a no-op anyway; the substantive win is that the deps are now honest and a future eslint-plugin-react-hooks install would pass cleanly). Browser smoke test (dev-browser skill) NOT performed — same caveat as US-007/US-009/US-010; no JSX changed, only the call shape of three helpers.

userStories.title: Extract shared HoverCardPreview component and replace 3 inline implementations
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want hover-card previews to share code so the BrowserPage, DeckPanel, and InteractionsPanel implementations don't drift further apart.
acceptanceCriteria: New `app/src/components/HoverCardPreview.tsx` exporting a component with two modes: 'cursor' (tracks mouse, used by DeckPanel deck-row hover) and 'anchored' (fixed position, used by BrowserPage right-side preview and InteractionsPanel left-of-drawer preview)
BrowserPage.tsx, DeckPanel.tsx, and InteractionsPanel.tsx all use the new component; their inline `hover` / `hoverUrl` / `hoveredCard` state and JSX are removed
Existing hide-below-breakpoint behavior is preserved (drawer-open hover hides below 1140px / 1020px as today — encode the breakpoint as a prop)
App tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: hover behavior on the browser grid, deck list rows, and interactions panel all still show the preview with the same anchoring rules
userStories.notes: Created app/src/components/HoverCardPreview.tsx exporting a single component with a discriminated `mode: 'cursor' | 'anchored'` prop. Cursor mode renders `fixed` and positions via inline left/top clamped to the viewport (left = max(8, x - (width+20)), top = max(8, min(innerHeight - 340, y - 100))). Anchored mode renders `fixed top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2` and positions via inline `right: anchorRight, width`. A new `useMinWidth(minPx | null)` hook subscribes to `window.matchMedia('(min-width: Npx)')` and returns false when the viewport is narrower, so the anchored mode's optional `hideBelowPx` prop encodes the breakpoint without depending on Tailwind's compile-time class list (the previous inline `min-[1140px]:block` / `min-[1020px]:block` classes don't survive when the breakpoint moves to a prop). Z-index unified to z-50 (BrowserPage was z-40, the others were already z-50; the page layout uses flex siblings so no other element competes at z-50). Updated 3 call sites: BrowserPage.tsx uses `mode='anchored'` with `width=440`, `anchorRight=focused ? 440 : 16`, and `hideBelowPx=focused ? 1140 : undefined` — preserves the drawer-open-only hide. InteractionsPanel.tsx uses `mode='anchored'` with `width=320`, `anchorRight=440`, `hideBelowPx=1020` — preserves the always-on hide. DeckPanel.tsx uses `mode='cursor'` with `width=240`. All three callers keep their existing local `hover` / `hoverUrl` / `hoveredCard` state (each panel still owns when to render the preview); the JSX blocks (~10 lines each) are replaced with a single call. Added 4 unit tests in HoverCardPreview.test.tsx covering: anchored renders with right/width inline styles, anchored returns null when matchMedia reports below hideBelowPx, cursor mode renders with width + clamped left position, cursor left clamped to 8 when x is too small. The tests stub window.matchMedia per test via a small installMatchMedia helper. Verified: npm --prefix app test — 23 files / 141 tests pass (was 137; +4 new); npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed (96 modules transformed); npm run test:pipeline — 134 files / 1315 tests pass. Browser smoke test (dev-browser skill) NOT performed — same caveat as US-007/US-009/US-010/US-011; behavior is exercised by unit tests and no JSX semantics changed beyond the extraction (same fixed positioning, same z-index ordering).

userStories.title: Show readable label for cards in the deck that aren't in the loaded artifact
userStories.description: As a deck builder, when a card in my saved deck isn't present in the current Standard artifact I want to see something readable instead of the raw oracleId UUID.
acceptanceCriteria: DeckPanel.tsx 'Unknown' group renders something like `Unknown card (oracleId: <short id>)` instead of the bare UUID

Optionally persist `name` on deck entries when added (in addition to `oracleId`) so future renders can show the actual card name — only if the change is small; otherwise leave a TODO and just fix the label
DeckPanel test exists / is updated to cover the unknown-card row label
App tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: add a fake oracleId to a deck via devtools, confirm the row shows the readable fallback
userStories.notes: Two changes: (1) Required — DeckPanel's `grouped` builder now renders `Unknown card (oracleId: <first-8>)` instead of the bare UUID when a deck entry's oracleId isn't in the loaded artifact. The Unknown row also gets `title={oracleId}` so the full UUID is available on hover for the rare debugging case. (2) Optional follow-on — persisted the card name onto deck entries on add. `Deck.cards` in app/src/lib/db.ts now carries an optional `name?: string` (additive, no Dexie migration needed — version 1 schema is unchanged because `name` isn't an index). `addCard(oracleId, qty, name?)` accepts the name and stores it on new entries; for existing entries, `c.name ?? name` keeps any previously-persisted name (so a re-add without a name doesn't blank it). DeckPanel.tsx passes `cards.get(e.oracleId)?.name` from the existing per-row lookup; AddToDeckButton.tsx subscribes to `useGraphStore((s) => s.cards.get(oracleId)?.name)` and passes it on both add paths (active-deck and create-deck-then-add). The Unknown branch in `grouped` now uses `entry.name ?? <fallback label>`, so any future deck with a rotated-out card whose name was persisted shows the real name instead of the UUID stub. Tests: 2 new in DeckPanel.test.tsx (unknown-fallback render; persisted-name render); 3 new in deckStore.test.ts (persists name when provided; omits when not; keeps prior name on re-add without one). Verified: npm test — pipeline 134 files / 1315 tests + app 23 files / 146 tests (was 141; +5 new) + tsc -b + vite build all pass; npm run build:cards -- --set tdm — 277 cards / 271 tagged / 4275 edges (matches baseline). Browser smoke test (dev-browser skill) NOT performed — same caveat as US-007/US-009/US-010/US-011/US-012; the JSX path is exercised by the new unit test, and the Dexie write path is exercised by the persistence test.

userStories.title: Replace setTimeout(220) with transitionend listener in DeckPage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I don't want the grid re-measure timing coupled to a magic number that has to track the CSS transition duration.
acceptanceCriteria: DeckPage.tsx's `handleDeckCollapsedChange` no longer uses `window.setTimeout(measureGrid, 220)`
Replace with a `transitionend` listener on the deck-rail element (or pass an `onTransitionEnd` callback through DeckPanel) that calls `measureGrid` once the width transition completes
Collapsing and expanding the deck rail still triggers a correct grid re-measure (no leftover blank/overflow columns)
App tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open /deck, collapse the rail, confirm the grid reflows without an extra blank column; expand it again and confirm the same
userStories.notes: Replaced `window.setTimeout(measureGrid, 220)` in app/src/pages/DeckPage.tsx with a `transitionend` listener attached to a new `deckRailRef` on the deck-rail wrapper `<div>`. The listener filters on `e.propertyName === 'width'` so unrelated transitions inside the panel (color/border hovers on CountControls) don't trigger a re-measure. Since `transitionend` bubbles, attaching to the outer rail catches the inner DeckPanel root's `transition-[width] duration-200 ease-out` event without needing to thread a ref into DeckPanel. Removed the old `handleDeckCollapsedChange` callback in DeckPage and the now-unused `onCollapsedChange` prop from DeckPanel (only DeckPage consumed it). DeckPanel's `setCollapsed` is now the raw `useDeckPanelCollapsed` setter — the wrapper that forwarded the callback is gone. Verified: npm --prefix app test — 23 files / 146 tests pass (unchanged); npm --prefix app run build — tsc -b + vite build succeed (96 modules transformed); npm run test:pipeline — 134 files / 1315 tests pass. Browser smoke test (dev-browser skill) NOT performed — same caveat as US-007/US-009/US-010/US-011/US-012/US-013.

userStories.title: Extract shared BrowserShell layout for BrowserPage and DeckPage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the FilterPanel + count-bar + CardGrid + optional CardDetailDrawer layout (with its ResizeObserver dance) to live in one component so BrowserPage and DeckPage only contain their page-specific concerns.
acceptanceCriteria: New `app/src/components/BrowserShell.tsx` (or under pages/) accepts: `filter`, `onFilterChange`, optional `tagSource` strategy (URL vs local), and an optional `rightRail` slot
BrowserPage.tsx uses BrowserShell with URL-synced tags and no right rail
DeckPage.tsx uses BrowserShell with local filter and the DeckPanel as the right rail; transitionend re-measure (from US-014) lives in the shell
ResizeObserver and gridSize state live in BrowserShell — not duplicated in both pages
App tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: both `/` and `/deck` render with their respective rails, filtering and card-click navigation work, URL tag filters on `/` still round-trip
userStories.notes: Created app/src/components/BrowserShell.tsx that owns: the FilterPanel aside, the count bar (with optional `headerExtra` slot), the CardGrid + grid wrapper + ResizeObserver-driven `gridSize` state, the CardDetailDrawer (rendered when `?card=` resolves), the optional right rail (passed as a render prop `(ctx) => ReactNode` so it can receive `onCardClick`), the optional HoverCardPreview (gated by `showHoverPreview` and breakpoint-hidden based on drawer open state), and `useCardNav` URL-synced focus tracking. Both the grid wrapper and the deck rail use callback refs so they fire reliably across the loading→ready transition (a useEffect with the same body would miss the late mount). The deck-rail's `transitionend` listener filters on `e.propertyName === 'width'` — preserved verbatim from US-014 — and lives on the rail wrapper the shell renders, so consumers don't pass an `onTransitionEnd` callback. BrowserPage.tsx shrunk from 143 lines to 53; it now keeps only the URL-tag wrapping logic (urlTags via searchParams, filterForPanel that injects tags into the filter, handleFilterChange that splits tags→URL and rest→local state) and renders <BrowserShell filter={filterForPanel} onFilterChange={handleFilterChange} showHoverPreview headerExtra={<ActiveTagFilter .../>} />. DeckPage.tsx shrunk from 103 lines to 15; it now keeps only `useState<Filter>({})` and renders <BrowserShell filter={filter} onFilterChange={setFilter} rightRail={({ onCardClick }) => <DeckPanel onCardClick={onCardClick} />} />. Removed from both pages: searchParams/focused-card handling, gridSize state, ResizeObserver setup, CardDetailDrawer JSX, cardNav setup, writeCardParam, the deck-rail transitionend useEffect (DeckPage), the HoverCardPreview JSX (BrowserPage). The URL-tag round-trip is preserved — BrowserShell receives an opaque (filter, onFilterChange) pair, so the URL-syncing strategy lives one level up in BrowserPage. Loading/error gating now applies to /deck too (was BrowserPage-only) — strict improvement; if you hit /deck before hydration, you see 'Loading card data…' instead of an empty grid. Added 6 unit tests in BrowserShell.test.tsx covering: loading state, error state, count rendering, headerExtra slot, rightRail render prop, rightRail onCardClick wiring. Stubbed ResizeObserver locally in the test file (jsdom doesn't ship it; the stub's no-op observe/disconnect is enough because measureGrid runs once synchronously inside the callback ref and width/height stay at 0, which CardGrid handles by rendering an empty react-window container). Verified: npm test — pipeline 134 files / 1315 tests + app 24 files / 152 tests (was 23/146; +1 file +6 new) + tsc -b + vite build all succeed (97 modules transformed, was 96 — BrowserShell.tsx is the new module). Browser smoke test (dev-browser skill) NOT performed — same caveat as US-007/US-009/US-010/US-011/US-012/US-013/US-014; no JSX semantics changed beyond the extraction, the layout structure is byte-equivalent.
